    <h1 {...{
      "id": "essential-development-tools-for-uxp"
    }}>{`Essential Development Tools for UXP`}</h1>
    <p>{`Let's explore the essential tools to start building UXP plugins`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "code-editor"
    }}>{`Code Editor`}</h2>
    <p>{`A reliable Code Editor helps you stay organized and productive. Any modern editor will work, but `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://code.visualstudio.com"
      }}>{`Visual Studio Code`}</a>{` is a popular choice thanks to its strong ecosystem, extensions for formatting and linting, and seamless integration with UXP-related workflows. `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://www.cursor.com/"
      }}>{`Cursor`}</a>{` is also a great option for developers who prefer a more AI-driven development experience.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "uxp-developer-tool-udt"
    }}>{`UXP Developer Tool (UDT)`}</h2>
    <p>{`The UXP Developer Tool (UDT) simplifies the process of creating, loading, managing, and debugging plugins for Adobe's UXP-powered applications. UXP plugins are supported in `}<strong parentName="p">{`Premiere v25.6`}</strong>{` and later since `}<strong parentName="p">{`UDT v2.2`}</strong>{`.`}</p>

    <p>{`In addition, the UXP Developer Tool:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Includes a `}<strong parentName="li">{`Code Playground`}</strong>{` to quickly test and explore APIs.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Lets you `}<strong parentName="li">{`package plugins`}</strong>{` into a `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`.ccx`}</inlineCode>{` `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../../plugins/distribution/package/index.md"
        }}>{`installable file`}</a>{`, ready for distribution.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Provides `}<strong parentName="li">{`Starter Templates`}</strong>{` and Sample Projects to help you get moving faster`}</li>
    </ul>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header,text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Admin privileges are required to use UDT.`}</p>
    <p>{`The UXP Developer Tool requires administrator-level privileges to run correctly. If you cannot elevate permissions on your system, you may not be able to use this tool.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "installation"
    }}>{`Installation`}</h3>
    <p>{`You can install UDT directly `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://creativecloud.adobe.com/apps/download/uxp-developer-tools"
      }}>{`from Creative Cloud`}</a>{` or by following these steps:`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Open the Adobe Creative Cloud desktop app. If you don't have it installed, `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://creativecloud.adobe.com/apps/download/creative-cloud"
        }}>{`download and install it here`}</a>{`.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Sign in with your Adobe ID if you haven't already.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Go to the `}<strong parentName="li">{`All apps`}</strong>{` section, where you'll find "UXP Developer Tools" listed; type "UXP Developer Tools" in the search bar for easier access.`}</li>

    <p>{`If you have problems with this step, you can also configure Developer Mode manually, although you must have Administrative privileges to do so.`}</p>
